title: German History Since 1949
description: A source-critical survey of the Federal Republic, the GDR, division, reunification, European integration, and social change since 1949.

modules.title: Peaceful Revolution and unity
objectives: Explain the Two Plus Four Treaty

sample_questions.question: Why was the Basic Law adopted in 1949 and initially called provisional?
sample_questions.reference_answer: The Parliamentary Council designed the Basic Law for the Federal Republic after dictatorship and war, with strong protection for human dignity and constitutional democracy. Its provisional language reflected the continued division of Germany and the goal of future unity.
facts: The Basic Law entered into force in 1949
German division influenced its provisional framing

sample_questions.question: Explain two causes and one consequence of the uprising of 17 June 1953.
sample_questions.reference_answer: Higher work norms and wider dissatisfaction with SED rule helped trigger protests in the GDR. Soviet forces suppressed the uprising, exposing the regime's dependence on Soviet power and deepening political division.
facts: Soviet forces helped suppress the uprising
The uprising occurred in the GDR

sample_questions.question: What changed with Ostpolitik in the early 1970s?
sample_questions.reference_answer: The Brandt government pursued practical normalization with the Soviet Union, Poland and the GDR, accepted existing borders in treaties, and sought change through rapprochement without abandoning the Federal Republic's Western alliances.
facts: It did not end West Germany's Western alignment

sample_questions.question: Why is 1989 described as a peaceful revolution in the GDR?
sample_questions.reference_answer: Mass demonstrations, civic groups, emigration pressure and reform demands undermined SED rule largely without violent overthrow. The opening of the Berlin Wall on 9 November became the best-known turning point.
facts: The Berlin Wall opened on 9 November 1989

sample_questions.question: What did the Two Plus Four Treaty settle?
sample_questions.reference_answer: The two German states and the four former occupying powers settled external aspects of German unity, including borders, sovereignty and security arrangements, enabling reunification on 3 October 1990.
facts: The treaty addressed external aspects of unity
